title: --log-driver=journald --log-opt tag does not work with podman-conmon
body:
From the podman RELEASE_NOTES.md

## 1.8.0
### Features
- Support for `--log-opt tag=` to set logging tags has been added to the `journald` log driver

podman version used is 3.4.4

This feature does not seem to work:

When I run:
#podman run --rm --log-driver=journald --log-opt tag="{{.ImageName}}/{{.Name}}/{{.ID}}" fedora echo "TEO was here"
                                                                               
Feb 10 15:51:12 localhost.localdomain systemd[1]: Started libpod-conmon-eff909743b4b2fee8e3cf6df5468e58986ee837013897b52a487c316983499b2.scope.                                                                                                         
Feb 10 15:51:12 localhost.localdomain systemd[1]: Started libcontainer container eff909743b4b2fee8e3cf6df5468e58986ee837013897b52a487c316983499b2.                                                                                                      
Feb 10 15:51:12 localhost.localdomain systemd[1]: Couldn't stat device /dev/char/10:200: No such file or directory                                                                                                                                      
**Feb 10 15:51:13 localhost.localdomain conmon[20419]: TEO was here**
Feb 10 15:51:13 localhost.localdomain systemd[1]: libpod-eff909743b4b2fee8e3cf6df5468e58986ee837013897b52a487c316983499b2.scope: Succeeded.       

I am expecting to have these tag on the conmon line.

Doing the same with docker:

Feb 10 14:50:03 localhost.localdomain kernel: IPv6: ADDRCONF(NETDEV_UP): eth0: link is not ready
Feb 10 14:50:03 localhost.localdomain kernel: IPv6: ADDRCONF(NETDEV_CHANGE): eth0: link becomes ready
Feb 10 14:50:03 localhost.localdomain kernel: IPv6: ADDRCONF(NETDEV_CHANGE): vethbf4b7b1: link becomes ready
Feb 10 14:50:03 localhost.localdomain kernel: docker0: port 1(vethbf4b7b1) entered blocking state
Feb 10 14:50:03 localhost.localdomain kernel: docker0: port 1(vethbf4b7b1) entered forwarding state
Feb 10 14:50:03 localhost.localdomain NetworkManager[685]: <info>  [1644504603.8928] device (vethbf4b7b1): carrier: link connected
Feb 10 14:50:03 localhost.localdomain NetworkManager[685]: <info>  [1644504603.8956] device (docker0): carrier: link connected
**Feb 10 14:50:03 localhost.localdomain _fedora/nifty_hamilton/3c34ab694aa7_[1545]: TEO was here**
Feb 10 14:50:04 localhost.localdomain dockerd[1545]: time="2022-02-10T14:50:04.045823688Z" level=info msg="ignoring event" container=3c34ab694aa73620d61fe8ca16bbf552ae86473926832ff5b544b4624fe177e5 module=libcontainerd namespace=moby topic=/tasks/delete type="*events.TaskDelete"
Feb 10 14:50:04 localhost.localdomain containerd[1534]: time="2022-02-10T14:50:04.072723027Z" level=info msg="shim disconnected" id=3c34ab694aa73620d61fe8ca16bbf552ae86473926832ff5b544b4624fe177e5

I try to use this feature to split the log of multiple containers running on the same host. All logs are coming from conmon but you cannot determine from what container. Using tags would allow to filter on these tags

comments.body: Could you verify if this is an issue with podman 4.0?
@rhatdan Latest version is 3.4, where can I get podman 4? Thx
4.0 is still in Release Candidate phase, but we should be making a final release this week.
Why did you close this issue?
Because, I thought it was stated that this works in Podman 4.0
FWIW I am using Podman 4.1.1 and it doesn't seem to be working.
I can confirm tags don't work with Debian stable's current versions of packages
